name: Grimlock
effect: Deploy a troop.
If an opponent causes you to discard this, draw 2 cards.
flavour: Destined to be born, live their lives, and then die in service to higher beings.

name: Cloaker
effect: Choose one:
• Place a spy.
• Return one of your spies ▶ Assassinate a troop at that spy's site.
flavour: "If you see a leather cape on the ground, stab it. Trust me." - Brawlwin Chainminer

name: Cranium Rats
effect: Deploy 2 troops.
Choose one opponent with more than 3 cards to discard a card.
flavour: A seed of malevolent intellect lurking in each rat, waiting for them to gather together.

name: Chuul
effect: Place a spy. Each opponent who has a troop at that spy's site and more than 3 cards, must discard a card.
flavour: They follow the ingrained directives of their creators, as they have done since the dawn of time.

name: Mindwitness
effect: Assassinate a troop. If that troop belonged to another player and they have more than 3 cards, they must discard a card.
flavour: "A beholder enslaved by mind flayers. Can you imagine the horrors that could be unleashed?" – Elminster Aumar

name: Ambassador
effect: At end of turn, promote another card played this turn.
If an opponent causes you to discard this, you may promote it instead.
flavour: It is important to forge new alliances, no matter how tenuous.

name: Nothic
effect: Choose one:
• Place a spy.
• Return one of your spies ▶ Draw a card. Each opponent with more than 3 cards must discard a card.
flavour: "You know something good. Something secret. I can see it, yes, yessss..."

name: Gauth
effect: Choose one:
• +2 influence
• Draw a card. Choose one opponent with more than 3 cards to discard a card.
flavour: If you value something, don't let a gauth see it.

name: Spectator
effect: +2 power, +1 influence
flavour: "If you were ripped from your home plane and forced to guard something for 101 years, you'd go a little mad too." – Volothamp Geddarm

name: Brainwashed Slave
effect: Choose one:
• Place a spy.
• Return one of your spies ▶ +2 power, +2 influence
flavour: It's mind belongs to something else. Something terrifying.

name: Umber Hulk
effect: Deploy 3 troops.
If an opponent causes you to discard this, they must discard a card.
flavour: Those lucky enough to survive an umber hulk attack often remember precious little of the incident, thanks to the umber hulk's mind-scrambling gaze.

name: Intellect Devourer
effect: Choose one:
• +3 influence
• Return up to two troops or spies.
flavour: Why fight your enemies, when you can so easily change their mind?

name: Quaggoth
effect: Assassinate one white troop for each site you control.
flavour: They maul their foes in a frenzy, becoming even more murderous in the face of death.

name: Puppeteer
effect: +2 influence
At end of turn, promote another card played this turn.
flavour: Sometimes you don't want to see who's pulling the strings.

name: Beholder
effect: Assassinate a troop.
Gain 1 power for every 3 troops in your trophy hall.
flavour: "Negotiating with a beholder is a task so dangerous, it is second only to spying on a Matron Mother." – Jarlaxle Baenre

name: Ulitharid
effect: Play a card in the market that costs 4 or less as if it was in your hand, then devour that card.
flavour: A rare illithid whose power and ambition rivals an elder brain.

name: Elder Brain
effect: Promote the top card of your deck.
Play a card from your inner-circle as if it were in your hand, then return it to your inner-circle.
flavour: It eagerly feasts on the brains of its illithid supplicants who desire to achieve "oneness" with their master.

name: Neogi
effect: Deploy 4 troops.
At end of turn, each opponent must discard a card.
flavour: Mind-controlling creatures from another plane that seek to enslave everything, even their own kind.

name: Aboleth
effect: Choose one
• Place 2 spies.
• Draw a card for each spy you have on the board.
flavour: Ancient and unknowable, these creatures will only accept complete submission.

name: Death Tyrant
effect: Assassinate up to 3 troops at a single site. For each troop removed gain 1 influence.
flavour: Nothing is worse than a beholder having nightmares about its mortality.
